mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which forces S&P 500 earnings down and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 70% of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, invalidating the labor deterioration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, lifting corporate revenues and generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A declining industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks that erode profit margins, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 65% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low policy rates combined with a falling unemployment rate create a liquidity-driven growth environment that compresses risk premiums and pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth surpassing 2.5% confirms robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases household income and consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns across all market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation alongside restrictive monetary policy triggers margin compression and growth fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: High inflation combined with rising rates fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.
